[{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

At the time of TAG submission the College of Biological Sciences requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA and at least a 2.50 GPA in each series with no grades less than a C for all majors to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.

","groupId":"af706f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":7,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission.

 

Important Information:


You must complete the courses below and maintain a 2.50 GPA in each series:

●     Biological Sciences (BIS) 002A/B/C

●     Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B/C or 004A/B/C

●     Mathematics (MAT) 017A/B/C or 021A/B


Highly Recommended Courses (not required for admission)


●     Organic Chemistry 8A/B or 118A/B/C

●     Physics 7A/B/C


Visit Department agreements as needed


Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (Find my advisor)


Note:

Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.

","groupId":"c5706f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":5,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

The Biochemistry & Molecular Biology major introduces students to the chemistry of living organisms and the experimental techniques that are used to probe the structures and functions of biologically important molecules. Students who enjoy both chemistry and biology and who are comfortable with quantitative approaches to problem solving will find this major a rewarding field of study.

","groupId":"d8706f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

The Biochemistry & Molecular Biology program provides a solid scientific background for students seeking a research, teaching, or service career in the life sciences. Positions are open to biochemists in bio-medical, biotechnological, pharmaceutical, agricultural research, and some chemical industries. Also, university-affiliated research laboratories, hospital laboratories, and government-sponsored research facilities provide employment opportunities. The major provides excellent preparation for advanced study in graduate or professional schools.
